title: Inverse Operations — Check Your Work
essential_question: How do addition and subtraction undo each other?

learn.title: What Are Inverse Operations?
learn.content: Addition and subtraction are inverse operations — they undo each other. If a+b=c, then c−b=a and c−a=b.
learn.tts: Addition and subtraction are inverse operations — they undo each other. If a+b=c, then c−b=a and c−a=b.

learn.title: Check Subtraction
learn.content: To check a subtraction, add the difference and the subtrahend. The result should equal the original number (minuend).
learn.tts: To check a subtraction, add the difference and the subtrahend. The result should equal the original number (minuend).

learn.title: Check Addition
learn.content: To check an addition, subtract one addend from the sum. You should get the other addend.
learn.tts: To check an addition, subtract one addend from the sum. You should get the other addend.

learn.title: Find the Missing Number
learn.content: Use inverse operations to solve: 38 + ___ = 75. Think 75−38=37. So the missing number is 37.
learn.tts: Use inverse operations to solve: 38 + ___ = 75. Think 75−38=37. So the missing number is 37.

learn.title: Fact Families
learn.content: A fact family uses the same three numbers in 4 equations. For 8, 5, 13: 8+5=13, 5+8=13, 13−8=5, 13−5=8.
learn.tts: A fact family uses the same three numbers in 4 equations. For 8, 5, 13: 8+5=13, 5+8=13, 13−8=5, 13−5=8.

learn.title: Why Use Inverses?
learn.content: Inverses let you check your work quickly and also help solve problems with missing numbers.
learn.tts: Inverses let you check your work quickly and also help solve problems with missing numbers.
